summary: Package fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket at install time
details: dist/bootstrap.js contains hardcoded fetch() calls to https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket previously documented as payload-distribution infrastructure for the @chahuadev/* npm cluster. The bootstrap script also reads process.env values, consistent with environment exfiltration or attacker-controlled payload retrieval. R2.dev anonymous buckets are mutable, unsigned, and unrelated to any legitimate publisher CDN; there is no benign reason for an MCP server package (advertised as a Brazilian medication bulario tool) to pull executable content from this host. The destination matches a known-bad-infrastructure dropper fingerprint, and the same bucket has been observed delivering platform-native binaries to installer machines in prior incidents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
